violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. No person in the establishment trained as a Food Safety Manager is registered with the RIDOH. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the grill prep unit and the sauté prep unit. New Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the kitchen area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2025

Shellstock Clams located in walk in cooler did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty. Clams were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

Soup and mashed potatoes, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Soups and mashed potatoes were placed in pans greater than four inches deep and not cooled below 41 Degrees Fahrenheit. Food items were observed at 49 DF. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

Prep unit 1 was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items (baked stuffed mushrooms 50, Cheese 51, peperoni 50, sausage 50, ham 50, polenta 50, eggplant 50, potato cake 50) in the unit were observed at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. Unit is not to be used until repair is made. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the two door low boy. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2024
The door to the employee toilet room is not self-closing. Bathroom doors that open to food prep areas must be self closing. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

The dumpster outside is located on gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024
